nodes.x: He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.
Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.

And therefore, he that will harden his heart, the same receiveth the lesser portion of the word; and he that will not harden his heart, to him is given the greater portion of the word, until it is given unto him to know the mysteries of God until he know them in full.
But because of priestcrafts and iniquities, they at Jerusalem will stiffen their necks against him, that he be crucified.
It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.
When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.
Nevertheless, when the wicked rule the people mourn.

While many thousands of others truly mourn for the loss of their kindred, yet they rejoice and exult in the hope, and even know, according to the promises of the Lord, that they are raised to dwell at the right hand of God, in a state of never-ending happiness.
In thy name shall they rejoice all the day: and in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.

the righteous mourn, because the wicked usurp authority, and exercise tyranny and oppression, and seem to go unpunished, no! for behold the day cometh and ‘the righteous shall be mine saith the Lord,* and in that day the wicked shall cease to trouble, and their names shall be blotted out from amongst men, and the weary and afflicted shall have rest and peace, and they shall enjoy the sweet, for…
When righteous men do rejoice, there is great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.
Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous men shameth his father.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known.
For in much wisdom is much grief: and he that increaseth knowledge increaseth sorrow.
The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.
And he gave battle unto Shule, the king, in which he did obtain the land of their first inheritance; and he became a king over that part of the land.
And that the Lord God would send or bring forth another people to possess the land, by his power, after the manner by which he brought their fathers.
And in his estate shall stand up a vile person, to whom they shall not give the honour of the kingdom: but he shall come in peaceably, and obtain the kingdom by flatteries.
But the king said unto him: Inquire of the Lord, and if he saith unto us go, we will go; otherwise we will perish in the land.
¶ And the Lord gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers; and they possessed it, and dwelt therein.
A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.
A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour; as he hath done, so shall it be done to him;
He that is first in his own cause seemeth just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him.
And he shall be received by the ordinance of the washing of feet, for unto this end was the ordinance of the washing of feet instituted.
In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
Thorns and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them.
The wicked have laid a snare for me: yet I erred not from thy precepts.
Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.
The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.
And thus he flattereth them, and leadeth them along until he draggeth their souls down to hell; and thus he causeth them to catch themselves in their own snare.
The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
He judged the cause of the poor and needy; then it was well with him: was not this to know me? saith the Lord.
He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.
the meek ; and the poor shall rejoice in the Holy One of Israel. And he that lendeth to the righteous poor shall be accounted a9 giving to the Lord.
He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.
For among my people are found wicked men: they lay wait, as he that setteth snares; they set a trap, they catch men.
In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.
If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.
A wise man feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.
He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed.
Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.
Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.
The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.
For the righteous Lord loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
Whoso causeth the righteous to go astray in an evil way, he shall fall himself into his own pit: but the upright shall have good things in possession.
The words of the wicked are to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.
A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.
Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.
A fool also is full of words: a man cannot tell what shall be; and what shall be after him, who can tell him?
The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.
If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.
But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.
They make the king glad with their wickedness, and the princes with their lies.
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the Lord lighteneth both their eyes.
The rich and poor meet together: the Lord is the maker of them all.
And the light which shineth, which giveth you light, is through him who enlighteneth your eyes, which is the same light that quickeneth your understandings;
He that hasteth to be rich hath an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.
He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.

annoying to borrow light from others, it is a disgrace to take a course in life to have to repent of the use made of that light. It is a disgrace to our organization, to the design of heaven, and to the intelligence God has given to man for his benefit. Truly wise persons hate to look upon such conduct, they look upon it with contempt. They are more worthy and noble than to condescend to take a…
The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.

Therefore I will be your king the remainder of my days; nevertheless, let us appoint judges, to judge this people according to our law; and we will newly arrange the affairs of this people, for we will appoint wise men to be judges, that will judge this people according to the commandments of God.
And thine house and thy kingdom shall be established for ever before thee: thy throne shall be established for ever.
And a mighty king shall stand up, that shall rule with great dominion, and do according to his will.
The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.
He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.

have lived in the sunbeams of parental kindness and affection, when made aware of a parent's displeasure, and receive a kind reproof from parental lips, are more thoroughly chastened, than by any physical punishment that could be applied to their persons. It is written, that the Lord “shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth.” And again it is written, “A whip for the horse, a bridle for…
A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.

Parents should never drive their children, but lead them along, giving
them knowledge as their minds are prepared to receive it. Chastening
may be necessary betimes, but parents should govern their children by
faith rather than by the rod, leading them kindly by good example into
all truth and holiness. 12:174.
When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.
But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake the wicked; and it is by the wicked that the wicked are punished; for it is the wicked that stir up the hearts of the children of men unto bloodshed.
The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.

Therefore, thou son of man, say unto the children of thy people, The righteousness of the righteous shall not deliver him in the day of his transgression: as for the wickedness of the wicked, he shall not fall thereby in the day that he turneth from his wickedness; neither shall the righteous be able to live for his righteousness in the day that he sinneth.
Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
Now behold, this giveth my soul sorrow; nevertheless, my soul hath joy in my son, because of that part of his seed which shall be taken unto God.
And I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
But, behold, my Beloved Son, which was my Beloved and Chosen from the beginning, said unto me—Father, thy will be done, and the glory be thine forever.
And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.
Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
But his delight is in the law of the Lord; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.
A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.

QUEST.         No one denies him
Skill in men, and skill to use them. His fault is
That in the ruler he forgets the servant,
As if he had been born to be commander.
And as a yearly hired servant shall he be with him: and the other shall not rule with rigour over him in thy sight.
Thou hast dealt well with thy servant, O Lord, according unto thy word.
Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
The words of wise men are heard in quiet more than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.
The simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.
He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
And because he dwelleth in flesh he shall be called the Son of God, and having subjected the flesh to the will of the Father, being the Father and the Son—
Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.

For verily, verily I say unto you, he that hath the spirit of contention is not of me, but is of the devil, who is the father of contention, and he stirreth up the hearts of men to contend with anger, one with another.
Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go:
A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
And the mean man shall be brought down, and the mighty man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the lofty shall be humbled:
And the mean man shall be brought down, and the mighty man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the lofty shall be humbled.
Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.
But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.
Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.
Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.
For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.
For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: but whosoever will lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it.
For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
¶ And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.
Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.
Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;
He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.
The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.
Ye that fear the Lord, trust in the Lord: he is their help and their shield.
Many seek the ruler’s favour; but every man’s judgment cometh from the Lord.
Let no man think he is ruler; but let God rule him that judgeth, according to the counsel of his own will, or, in other words, him that counseleth or sitteth upon the judgment seat.
But the Lord shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.
An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord: but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
Righteousness keepeth him that is upright in the way: but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.
Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the Lord.
Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
